articles.title: T. Rex Bones Reveal Hidden Blood Vessels!
articles.summary: Scientists found blood vessels inside the bones of a T. rex named Scotty. Usually, only hard parts like bones and teeth are found from dinosaurs. But sometimes, soft parts like blood vessels can be preserved. This is very rare and exciting because it tells us more about how dinosaurs lived. Scotty was a huge T. rex that lived 66 million years ago. It had a broken rib that was healing. When bones heal, more blood vessels grow there. Scientists used special X-rays to see inside the bone without breaking it. They saw tiny blood vessels that were turned into minerals. This helps us understand how dinosaurs healed from injuries. The discovery shows that even after millions of years, we can still find traces of dinosaur soft parts.

articles.title: New DNA Study Changes How We Think About Where Humans Came From
articles.summary: A new study about human origins says that early humans did not all come from one group in Africa. Instead, there were many groups of early humans living in different parts of Africa. They moved around and mixed with each other for a very long time. Scientists studied the DNA of people living today, especially the Nama people from southern Africa. The Nama have a lot of genetic variety. The study found that the first big split between early human groups happened about 120,000 to 135,000 years ago. Before that, different groups had been sharing genes for hundreds of thousands of years. This new idea is like a network instead of a simple family tree. It helps explain why humans have so much genetic diversity. The study was done by researchers at the University of California, Davis and McGill University. They say this changes our understanding of human evolution.

articles.title: Living in a Shed Because of River Pollution
articles.summary: A couple in Herefordshire, England, has been living in a shed for years because of river pollution. Jane and Tony Coyle wanted to build a new home, but they had to wait for special permission because the river Lugg is polluted. The pollution comes from farms and sewage. The rules to protect the river have delayed their building plans, costing them a lot of money and causing health problems. They are now part of a legal case against the companies that cause the pollution. Many other people are also affected because new homes cannot be built. The couple hopes their story will help clean up the rivers and allow people to build homes again.

articles.title: Fish Oil Might Not Be Good for Your Brain After Injury
articles.summary: Many people take fish oil supplements because they think it helps their brain. But a new study says fish oil might actually make it harder for the brain to heal after getting hurt. Scientists from the Medical University of South Carolina studied how fish oil affects the brain after injuries like concussions. They found that a part of fish oil called EPA can slow down the repair of blood vessels in the brain. This means that if you have a brain injury, taking fish oil could make things worse. The study used mice and human cells to see what happens. They also looked at brain tissue from people who had many head injuries. The scientists say not all parts of fish oil are bad—DHA, another part, is still good for the brain. But EPA might be harmful after an injury. So, it's important to be careful with fish oil supplements, especially if you play sports or have had head injuries.

articles.title: Animals Move Into New Wetland Home
articles.summary: A new wetland has been built at the Great Fen nature reserve in Cambridgeshire. It took one year to make and is as big as 60 football fields. The wetland has water, channels, and special plants called reedbeds. Birds like lapwings are already coming to look for food there. The project is part of a bigger plan to connect two old wetlands. The work is paid for by the National Lottery Heritage Fund. The wetland will be open to people in the summer. The project manager, Lorna Parker, is happy to see nature take over. Rain has helped fill the water areas. Muddy edges are good for insects and small animals, which birds can eat. This place will let people see nature returning after more than 100 years.

articles.title: A Brave Orangutan Crosses a New Bridge
articles.summary: In Indonesia, a road was built through a forest where orangutans live. This split the orangutan community into two groups. Scientists worried that if the groups could not meet, they might have health problems from inbreeding and could even disappear forever. To help, conservation groups and the government built a special bridge high up in the trees, called a canopy bridge. They hoped the orangutans would use it to cross the road safely. For two years, no orangutan used the bridge. But then, one brave orangutan finally crossed it! This was very exciting for the people who built the bridge. Now, the orangutans can visit each other again, which will help keep the community healthy.
